edibility: The most cultivated mushroom worldwide; eaten raw or cooked; some sources caution about raw agaritine but cooked is universally accepted.
taste: Mild, slightly earthy when raw; deeper umami when cooked; cremini and portobello varieties offer more intense flavors.
search: Wild form A. campestris exists in pastures; cultivated form mostly purchased from grocery stores. ID clues include white cap, white gills that later darken, and bulbous base. Dangerous look-alikes include Amanita bisporigera and Agaricus xanthodermus (yellow stainer).
